definition: Rolling stock-bond correlation at +0.15 to +0.35 is observable, driven by a three-force decomposition: AI capex demand-pull, no TFP supply response, and fiscal dominance. This implies 60/40 portfolios carry excess equity-equivalent risk, though the AI-specific contribution cannot be isolated from monetary, fiscal, and geopolitical drivers.
evidence: Rolling correlation at +0.15 to +0.35 is observable
Range of -0.2 to +0.4 post-2022 is within historical variation in non-AI periods
20-30% excess risk estimate challenged as unverifiable without conditioning on leverage and rebalancing
open_questions: Whether the positive correlation regime is primarily AI-driven or fiscal/monetary-driven
How to decompose portfolio risk between structural and cyclical correlation changes
Whether hedging alternatives exist at reasonable cost
